*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
[TO SOLVE THIS PROBLEM, WE FIRST NEED TO DETERMINE THE TIME ALLOCATED PER QUESTION FOR EACH TEST.]  

*HIGHLIGHTED SECTION*  
[WE THEN CALCULATE THE DIFFERENCE IN TIME PER QUESTION BETWEEN THE TWO TESTS.]  

[TO FIND THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY, DIVIDE THE TOTAL TIME (IN SECONDS) BY THE NUMBER OF QUESTIONS.]  
[TO FIND THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY, DIVIDE THE TOTAL TIME (IN SECONDS) BY THE NUMBER OF QUESTIONS.]  
[SUBTRACT THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY FROM THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY TO FIND THE DIFFERENCE.]  

[THE TOTAL TIME FOR BIOLOGY IS 45 MINUTES, WHICH IS 2700 SECONDS.]  
[THE TOTAL TIME FOR CHEMISTRY IS 60 MINUTES, WHICH IS 3600 SECONDS.]  
[THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR BIOLOGY IS 2700 SECONDS DIVIDED BY 25 QUESTIONS, EQUALING 108 SECONDS.]  
[THE TIME PER QUESTION FOR CHEMISTRY IS 3600 SECONDS DIVIDED BY 20 QUESTIONS, EQUALING 180 SECONDS.]  
[THE DIFFERENCE IN TIME PER QUESTION IS 180 SECONDS MINUS 108 SECONDS, EQUALING 72 SECONDS.]  

[THE ANSWER IS 72 SECONDS.]
